---
title: "Social Learning, Crowds & Your Project"
subtitle: "RIKEN Wako Science Camp 2026 — Day 3"
author: "Computational Group Dynamics (COGNAC) Collaboration Unit, RIKEN CBS"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default]
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    transition: slide
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Today {.center}

**Linked hands-on notebooks（演習用ノートブック）:**<br>
`tutorials/04_social_learning_notebook.Rmd`<br>
`tutorials/05_collective_behaviour_notebook.Rmd`

You built an agent that **learns alone**.

Today: agents that **watch each other**.

Societies appear. They can be wise, or they can make mistakes.

## Why copy others?

New town, hungry. One ramen shop has a queue, one is empty.

::: {.fragment}
Other people's choices carry **information**.

Copying is often *smart* — humans are champions of
**social learning**（社会学習）. It gave us culture,
language, science... and TikTok.
:::

## Two knobs of a social learner {.smaller}

$$
p(\text{choice})
= (1-\sigma)\,p_{\text{individual}}
+ \sigma\,p_{\text{social}}
$$

- $p_{\text{individual}}$: your own experience
- $p_{\text{social}}$: other people's choices
- $\sigma$: **how much** to rely on others (0 → 1)
$$
p_{\text{social}} \propto \text{count}^{\theta}
$$

- $\theta$: **how strongly** to follow majorities
  - $\theta = 1$: copy in proportion
  - $\theta > 1$: **conformity**（同調）— exaggerate the majority


::: {.fragment}
Different values = different *social strategies*.
Which strategy is the smartest?
:::

## Hands-on 1: Tutorial 4 {.center}

Open:

`tutorials/04_social_learning_notebook.Rmd`

Build a society of learners. Change $\sigma$ (sigma) and $\theta$ (theta).

**Predict first. Then run.**
